# Avatar

Displays a person or entity image with a reliable fallback.

## Installation

Add the Avatar component from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/avatar
```

## Usage

Compose `AvatarImage` and `AvatarFallback` inside `Avatar`. The fallback appears while the image is unavailable or fails to load, preventing an empty or broken image from replacing a person's identity.

**Avatar example**

```vue
<template>
  <Avatar>
    <AvatarImage
      src="https://github.com/stacktracelabs.png"
      alt="StackTrace Labs"
    />
    <AvatarFallback>ST</AvatarFallback>
  </Avatar>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import {
  Avatar,
  AvatarFallback,
  AvatarImage,
} from '@/Components/Avatar'
</script>
```

## Fallback content

Use short, recognizable fallback content such as one or two initials. Keep its background, dimensions, and shape aligned with the image so loading does not shift nearby content. `AvatarFallback` forwards Reka's `delay-ms` prop when a brief delay would prevent the fallback from flashing on fast connections.

```vue
<Avatar>
  <AvatarImage src="/people/ana.jpg" alt="Ana Novak" />
  <AvatarFallback :delay-ms="200">AN</AvatarFallback>
</Avatar>
```

## Sizing and shape

The default avatar is circular and `size-8`. Apply the same size and radius expectations to the root rather than styling the image and fallback independently. Use larger avatars only when recognizing the person is important; pair small avatars with visible names in dense lists.

## Images and accessible names

When the image is the only identification, provide an `alt` value that names the person or entity. When a visible name immediately beside it already provides the same information, use an empty `alt` value so the name is not announced twice. Do not put words such as “avatar” or “profile picture” in the alternative text.

Initials are a visual fallback, not a complete accessible identity. Keep the visible name in the interface or provide an accessible name on the surrounding control when the avatar is used inside a button. Never use the avatar alone to communicate presence, permission, or another status.

## When to use

Use Avatar for people, teams, or recognizable entities. Use a normal decorative image component for product photography and illustrations. When an avatar opens a menu, wrap it in a properly labeled interactive trigger; the Avatar itself is presentational and does not add button behavior.

Hosted documentation: [Avatar](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/avatar)
